sourcePrompt: A traditional Vietnamese village hut of the Mekong Delta, a single small room about four meters wide, raised a hand's height off the ground on short wooden posts. Woven bamboo-mat walls under a steep thatched palm-leaf roof whose eaves overhang deeply, a single doorway with a woven door leaf, and one small window opening. Weathered bamboo and sun-bleached golden thatch, humble and tidy.
handEdit: roof thatch rotation signs flipped to lie on slope planes, panels raised 0.13 to meet rafters/hold-downs, top courses shortened under ridge cap; window leaf flipped onto its prop stick; offline rebake
